The input directory containing all DWI images
Input directory containing brain masks, corresponding to one per input image (with the same file name prefix)
The output directory containing all of the intensity normalised DWI images
The output population specific FA template, which is threshold to estimate a white matter mask
The output white matter mask (in template space), used to estimate the median b=0 white matter value for normalisation
The threshold applied to the Fractional Anisotropy group template used to derive an approximate white matter mask (default: 0.4)

mrtrix.synopsis: Perform various forms of intensity normalisation of DWIs
description: This script provides access to different techniques for globally scaling the intensity of diffusion-weighted images. The different algorithms have different purposes, and different requirements with respect to the data with which they must be provided & will produce as output. Further information on the individual algorithms available can be accessed via their individual help pages; eg. "dwinormalise group -help".

group.synopsis: Performs a global DWI intensity normalisation on a group of subjects using the median b=0 white matter value as the reference
description: The white matter mask is estimated from a population average FA template then warped back to each subject to perform the intensity normalisation. Note that bias field correction should be performed prior to this step.
All input DWI files must contain an embedded diffusion gradient table; for this reason, these images must all be in either .mif or .mif.gz format.

description:
Perform various forms of intensity normalisation of DWIs.

This script provides access to different techniques for globally scaling the intensity of diffusion-weighted images. The different algorithms have different purposes, and different requirements with respect to the data with which they must be provided & will produce as output. Further information on the individual algorithms available can be accessed via their individual help pages; eg. "dwinormalise group -help".
